What it does: Basic attack on the aimed enemy.
Clarify more: Mechanically this IS a homing projectile - fired at the aim target and always landing on it, which is why its Collision is Target-Only. It is kept as its own Action only because it is the unit's default attack rather than an ability. NOT always performed by the champion: read the Action Source column. Azir's Sand Soldier auto-attacks (Source = Summon), and Sejuani's passive rides on an ALLY's auto-attack (Source = Ally).
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Spread, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: The champion auto-attacks the target several times in QUICK SUCCESSION - N basic-attack-like hits in a row, not a real ability cast.
Clarify more: Bel'Veth: 6/6/25 rapid attacks on the lowest-HP enemy. Mechanically like Auto-Attack (Target-Only homing) but fired as a burst of N via Count, so it is its own Action rather than Auto-Attack (the unit's DEFAULT attack).
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: Fires a projectile that homes on the aim target and applies its effect to it.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. Nothing on the way is touched - the projectile tracks the target until it lands (Poppy, Cassiopeia, Samira). Kalista's 6 spears are 6 of these at one target.
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Spread, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: Fires a projectile that stops on the FIRST unit it hits.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. The first unit it collides with may NOT be the unit aimed at - that is the whole point: Taliyah's boulder is thrown toward a knocked-up enemy but hits whatever it meets first, and each of Akshan's 6 shots lands on the first body in its lane.
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Spread, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: Fires a projectile that passes THROUGH every unit in its path, hitting all of them.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. Recipients can be enemies or allies - Sona's damages enemies AND buffs allies in the same path. Replaced the old 'Wave', which was a duplicate of it.
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Spread, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: Projectile that flies at the aim target and DETONATES on impact, hitting everyone in an X-hex circle.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. = First hit Projectile + Circle AOE. It detonates on the FIRST body it meets, which may not be the unit it was aimed at, and the circle is centred on that IMPACT hex. If the projectile instead passes THROUGH and detonates on its aim target, that is Homing Burst Projectile (Aphelios), not this.
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Spread, Collision, AOE, Skill Range

What it does: Projectile that HOMES on the aim target, passing through everything on the way, and detonates on it in an X-hex circle.
Clarify more: Has travel speed. = Homing Projectile + Circle AOE. Do NOT confuse with Burst Projectile, which is First-Hit: that one detonates on the FIRST body it meets, which may not be the unit it was aimed at. Aphelios' moon blast passes THROUGH heroes and explodes on the clustered target it was aimed at - the user's correction. Same circle, different delivery.
Columns used: Aim Target, Count, Spread, Collision, AOE, Skill Range

What it does: Waits for a previously thrown projectile to RETURN to the caster, then applies its effect to self.
Clarify more: Collision is Self, NOT None: something IS flying back and the unit it collides with is the caster (Poppy's shield).

Delivery (where it spawns / does it trav: Spawn At Target (spawns on target, no travel)
What it does: The hitbox appears directly ON the aim target's hex - nothing is fired and nothing travels.
Clarify more: Single-target on-target spawn only (Taliyah). The AOE-shaped 'spawn on target' cases moved to the shape templates (Circle/Box/Custom AOE), whose centre is derived from Aim Target the same way — nothing travels, so nothing between is touched.

Delivery (where it spawns / does it trav: Spawn At Target (spawns on target, no travel)
What it does: Affects everyone inside an X-hex circle (X = the AOE (hex) column).
Clarify more: Centre is DERIVED from Action Source + Aim Target — the caster's hex when Aim=Self (Garen, Galio), the target's hex otherwise (Karma, Vi, Yasuo). The Offset column places the circle relative to that centre (usually 'centred'). Replaced the old 'Melee AOE' idea: a self-aimed Circle AOE already IS the melee case.
Columns used: Aim Target, Offset, AOE, Collision, Skill Range

Delivery (where it spawns / does it trav: Spawn on unit (toggles on cast, no travel)
What it does: A cone-shaped hitbox projected in front of the caster, switched on when the skill is cast; everyone inside the cone is hit.
Clarify more: Kassadin, Aatrox. A real cone-shaped collider (Collision Area, Shape Cone). Centre derived from Action Source + Aim Target; the Offset column is normally 'front edge' because the cone projects forward from that centre.
Columns used: Aim Target, Offset, AOE, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: A rectangular AOE hitbox. Size in AOE (hex); the Offset column says where the box sits relative to its centre (centred / rear edge / front edge / detached +N).
Clarify more: Aatrox's transformed 1×2 box. One delivery with Circle/Cone — they differ only in Shape and the AOE size; centre is derived from Action Source + Aim Target.
Columns used: Aim Target, Offset, AOE, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: An irregular AOE that does not fit Circle/Cone/Box; its shape is described in the AOE (hex) cell. Centre derived from Action Source + Aim Target; Offset as usual.
Clarify more: Miss Fortune's X-shaped rain. Use only for genuinely odd shapes.
Columns used: Aim Target, Offset, AOE, Collision, Skill Range

What it does: Beam that pierces ALL units in the path but applies its effect ONCE.
Clarify more: NO travel speed - it spawns on the unit and points at the target. Irelia's frontal hit, Senna's board-wide beam, and Viego's strike are all this.

What it does: A laser that SWEEPS sideways through an arc while it is out, hitting everything the moving beam passes over.
Clarify more: Gwen. The cone she appears to make is NOT a cone hitbox - it is this line sweeping, and it only LOOKS like a cone. This is the first action whose hitbox MOVES during the action: Delivery says where a hitbox spawns and Shape says what it is, but neither can say that it travels sideways. Carried in the Shape text for now; it deserves its own column if a second swept action ever appears. Replaced 'Cone Slash', which wrongly modelled the cone as a real collider.

What it does: Beam that pierces ALL units in the path and damages them continuously until it ends.
Clarify more: NO travel speed. Taxonomy only - no Set 9 unit here uses it.

What it does: Beam locked onto the aimed target ONLY, applying its effect over a duration.
Clarify more: NO travel speed. Nothing between the unit and the target is touched (Lux).

What it does: Self CHARGES to the target hex, colliding with every unit in the path, and stops there.
Clarify more: The CASTER's own body is the hitbox. Not an AOE - the units hit are exactly the ones it ran through (Sion).

What it does: Self smashes the aimed enemy across the board.
Clarify more: The FLYING ENEMY is the hitbox: everyone it collides with on the way is hit as well. Not a projectile the caster fired - the thrown body IS the projectile (K'Sante).

What it does: Self grabs one enemy from EACH side and slams them together.
Clarify more: The effect applies only to the grabbed enemies. If only one side is occupied then only one is grabbed - and the effect is amplified by 50% (Sett).

What it does: Self leaps to the target hex - a reposition.
Clarify more: Projects nothing, so Collision is None. Normally just sets up the follow-up action that does the damage (Jarvan's and Katarina's landing AOE; Gwen's dash before her sweep).

What it does: Self leaps to the hex directly BEHIND the current target.
Clarify more: Rek'Sai - she rips 1 hex through the target and ends up behind it. Distinct from Leap, which lands on a chosen/aimed hex; this one is defined RELATIVE to the current target, not a free hex.

What it does: Self plays an animation, then applies the stated effect or sets up the step that follows.
Clarify more: Projects no hitbox into the world, so its Collision is ALWAYS None - even when it is a melee strike on a target (Darius, Yasuo). Who it lands on is carried by Aim Target and Effect Recipient, not by the collision. Beware: a 'melee strike' is not automatically a Cast - Viego looked like one and is actually a Laser Shot.

What it does: Self spawns a unit (a clone, a soldier) at a target hex.
Clarify more: The spawned unit becomes a SECOND action source for the steps that follow - it acts on its own, and its action is centred on IT, not on Self. Zed's Shadow and Azir's Sand Soldier.

Action: This tab is the single definition of every Action. It replaced 'Action Types' and 'Action Templates', which described the same actions in two places - one of them less precisely - and that is how two tabs drift apart until nobody knows which is true. Both are now deleted.

An Action is not a primitive - it is a COMPOSITION: Delivery x Collision x Shape. That is why Burst Projectile = First hit Projectile + Circle AOE, and why Circle AOE has the SAME delivery as Spawn At Target and differs only in shape.
Count and Spread add a fourth axis - how MANY instances fire and how they are arranged. Those are columns in the Hero tab, and their values are defined in 'Spread Types'.

KNOWN GAP: nothing here can say that a hitbox MOVES while the action runs. Sweep Laser (Gwen) is the only case so far and carries it in the Shape text. A second swept action should turn that into a real column rather than prose.
